data.title: Carnival Stock (CCL): Here's Why Analysts Are Lowering their Price Targets Ahead of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Cruise operator Carnival Corporation (CCL) is scheduled to announce its results for the first quarter of Fiscal 2026 on March 27. CCL stock has declined 19% over the past month due to a spike in oil prices amid the U.S.-Iran conflict. Ahead of Q1 FY26 earnings, several analysts have lowered their price targets for CCL stock to reflect elevated fuel prices. However, most analysts remain bullish on Carnival's long-term growth prospects and see attractive upside in the stock.

Meanwhile, the consensus adjusted earnings per share estimate of $0.19 for Q1 FY26 reflects 46% year-over-year growth. Carnival's fiscal first-quarter revenue is expected to grow 5.7% to $6.14 billion.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: GME Earnings: GameStop Stock Gains after Topping Q4 Profit Expectations
data.description:
GameStop's (GME) fourth‑quarter results show stronger profitability and tighter cost controls, even as revenue declined year over year. The video game retailer reported adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.49, which topped the $0.37 consensus estimate and compares favorably with $0.30 in the prior-year quarter.

Meanwhile, Q4 revenue totaled $1.1 billion, which missed the $1.47 billion forecast by Wall Street analysts. Sales were down 14% from a year earlier, reflecting continued softness in software and hardware demand.

Is GME Stock a Buy?

TipRanks' AI Analyst maintained a Neutral rating on the stock with a price target of $25.00 per share. The price target suggests about 9.22% upside from current levels. According to TipRanks' A.I. Stock Analysis, GameStop stock scores 62 out of 100.

The model highlights "improving profitability, stronger cash flow, and a healthier balance sheet" as positives, but weak technical trends and valuation concerns keep the outlook cautious.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: 'Bitcoin May Have Found a Bottom,' Says Bernstein
data.description:
Bitcoin may have finally found a bottom after months of decline, according to Bernstein analysts. The cryptocurrency has been trading in a tight range between about $65,000 and $75,000 over the past few weeks and was below $70,000 on Tuesday. After falling roughly 20% this year and dropping from its $126,000 peak in October, many investors have been hesitant to call a turning point. That said, Bernstein believes the worst is likely behind it and continues to target $150,000 by the end of 2026.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, Bernstein's view is being driven by a shift in key demand trends. For instance, Bitcoin ETFs, which saw money flowing out earlier this year, are now seeing inflows again and currently hold around 6.1% of the total supply. On top of that, Strategy (MSTR) has been a major buyer and now holds roughly 3.6% of all Bitcoin. This steady accumulation is helping support prices, and Bernstein sees Strategy as a high-upside way to gain exposure. Indeed, Bernstein's five-star-rated Gautam Chhugani recently maintained a Buy rating with a $450 price target.

In addition, Bitcoin has actually outperformed some traditional assets during recent geopolitical tensions, rising more than 6% since the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran began, while gold has fallen by about 15%. It also moved higher alongside stocks after President Trump pointed to possible talks with Iran. Still, not all analysts are convinced that this is a confirmed bottom, with some arguing that the recent bounce lacks a strong follow-through.

Is Bitcoin a Good Buy?

Using TipRanks' technical analysis tool, the indicators seem to point to a negative outlook for Bitcoin. Indeed, the summary section pictured below shows that five indicators are Bullish, compared to four Neutral and 13 Bearish indicators.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: QEW ETF: Steady Start for Invesco's New Equal-Weight Nasdaq-100 Fund in 2026
data.description:
The Invesco QQQ Equal Weight ETF (QEW) is one of the newest additions to Invesco's offerings, launched on March 18, 2026. It is an equal-weighted alternative to the traditional Nasdaq‑100 Index (NDX). It tracks the Nasdaq-100 Equal Weighted Index, giving each company the same influence regardless of size, and carries a 0.25% expense ratio.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade TSLA with leverage
  • New trading tool for TSLA bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

Since its debut, QEW has traded in a stable range, hovering between $24.34 and $25.09. Today, the ETF traded around $24.56, down about 0.8%, reflecting a modest but steady start for a newly listed fund.

Why Are Investors Paying Attention?

The new ETF aims to give investors a more balanced approach to mega‑cap tech exposure by spreading weight evenly across all index constituents rather than concentrating heavily in the largest names such as NVIDIA (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), and Tesla (TSLA).

This equal‑weight structure can be helpful for investors in periods when mid‑tier Nasdaq names outperform the mega‑caps, when market leadership broadens beyond the top handful of tech giants, or when investors are looking to reduce concentration risk within the Nasdaq‑100.

Investors Looking for Exposure to Similar ETFs

Direxion NASDAQ-100 Equal Weighted Index Shares (QQQE) and First Trust Nasdaq-100 Equal Weighted Index Fund (QQEW) are two ETFs that compare closely with Invesco's newly launched QEW ETF.

Like QEW, QQEW and QQQE spread weight evenly across all Nasdaq‑100 constituents, reducing reliance on mega‑caps and giving mid‑tier names more influence on performance.

Using the TipRanks ETF Comparison tool, let's look at which ETF Wall Street prefers.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Trump's Approval Rating Slumps to Term Low amid Growing Unrest over Iran War
data.description:
President Trump's approval rating has fallen to a second-term low of 36%, according to a Reuters/Ipsos poll that collected responses from 1,272 American adults between March 20 and 23. His lowest approval during his first term was 33%.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

35% of Americans are in favor of U.S. strikes on Iran, down from 37% in the prior week but still higher than the 27% seen during the start of the war. Since then, gas prices have surged by $1, or 33%, to $3.98.

Trump's Approval Rating Drops as Economic Concerns Deepen

Furthermore, 25% of respondents approved of Trump's handling of the cost of living, while 29% approved of his economic stewardship, marking the lowest rating for either of his terms.

"This provides a huge opportunity for Democrats to make major strides in the midterms by focusing on issues that are traditionally more Republican issues, like national security, the economy, and immigration," said Democratic strategist Doug Farrar.

Meanwhile, 26% believe the war will make the U.S. safer in the long run, while 38% say that it won't have a material effect on safety. In recent days, Trump has hinted at the war winding down, although the arrival of U.S. troops in the Middle East risks escalation between the two sides.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Coca-Cola (KO) Is Warren Buffett's Best Dividend Stock
data.description:
Famed investor Warren Buffett loves dividend stocks. Nearly every stock in Berkshire Hathaway's (BRK.B) portfolio pays a dividend. And among those stocks, Coca-Cola (KO) has been the best dividend payer.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Buffett has held 400 million shares of KO stock since the early 1990s. He hasn't touched the holding in more than 30 years. Instead, he has continued to collect an endless stream of dividend payments from the beverage giant.

In 1994, when Berkshire Hathaway finished buildings its position in Coca-Cola, it received $75 million in cash dividends from KO stock. This year, Berkshire will earn $848 million in dividends from its holding of Coca-Cola's stock. Not bad for doing nothing.

What Makes KO a Great Dividend Stock

Coca-Cola has raised its dividend for 64 consecutive years, making it a "Dividend King," a title given to companies that increase their distribution to shareholders for more than 50 years in a row. Currently, KO stock pays a quarterly dividend of $0.53 a share, giving it a yield of nearly 3%.

However, given how long Berkshire Hathaway has owned Coke's stock, its cumulative dividend yield is about 60%. Buffett has attributed dividend growth to the magic of compounding. Of Coca-Cola's dividend growth over the years, Buffett famously said: "Growth occurred every year, just as certain as birthdays… All we were required to do was cash Coke's quarterly dividend checks."

Is KO Stock a Buy?

Coca-Cola's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 15 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average KO price target of $85.07 implies 8.70% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Slips Despite Newest FAA Approval
data.description:
We know that the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has quite a few designs seeking approval in its backlog, including several from aerospace stock Boeing (BA). While many of us are waiting for the approval to land for the 777X lineup, one other approval did show up. Specifically, it was a modification for the 787-9 and 787-10 widebody line. This did oddly little to perk up shareholder opinion, as Boeing shares slipped fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The FAA approved new increased maximum takeoff weight (iMTOW) figures for the 787-9 and 787-10 recently. This is the first major certification Boeing has won from the FAA since March 2021, reports note. Back then, the FAA gave approval to the 737 MAX 8200, which was a version of the 737-8 that offered a new exit door.

Under the new certification, the 787-9 can now take off with 259.2 metric tons, reports note, and the 787-10 got an increase to 260.3 metric tons. That extra tonnage can go to carry weight, or it can increase range by either 310 nautical miles for the 787-9 or 430 nautical miles for the 787-10.

Coolest Thing in South Carolina

Meanwhile, Boeing also found itself beneficiary of some noteworthy news, as a new poll emerged from South Carolina. After counting roughly 35,000 votes, the viewers of WCIV declared the Boeing 787 the Coolest Thing Made in South Carolina.

There were four finalists in the running for the title, including Boeing, as well as the Constant Velocity Joint by JTEKT, Zeus' PFX Flex Sub-Lite-Wall, and the CardioEducator by NursEdvance Innovations. The annual competition ended with votes cast and Governor Henry McMaster delivering the decree of the winner, backed up by a slate of South Carolina government officials.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 13 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 8.66% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $280.27 per share implies 42.779% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Polymarket Odds: Will PDD, CHWY, CTAS Beat Earnings Tomorrow?
data.description:
On Wednesday, March 24, Pinduoduo (PDD), Chewy (CHWY), and Cintas (CTAS) will report their earnings. All three companies will report after the closing bell. Traders are already weighing in with their expectations on Polymarket.

Wall Street's Earnings Estimates

  • Pinduoduo is expected to report an adjusted EPS of $3.06.
  • Chewy is expected to report an adjusted EPS of 28 cents.
  • Cintas is expected to report a GAAP EPS of $1.23.

What is Polymarket?

Polymarket is a prediction platform where traders bet on the outcomes of real-world events, including earnings reports, elections, and economic indicators. Polymarket odds reflect expectations and can provide insight into investor sentiment.

Will Pinduoduo Beat Earnings?

Pinduoduo has beaten or met earnings estimates during 14 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 77.77%. Polymarket gives the e-commerce company a 72% chance of beating its estimated adjusted EPS of $3.06.

Will Chewy Beat Earnings?

Chewy has beaten or met earnings estimates during 13 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 72.22%. Polymarket gives the pet supplies company a 92% chance of beating its estimated adjusted EPS of 28 cents.

Will Cintas Beat Earnings?

Cintas has beaten or met earnings estimates during 18 of the past 18 quarters for a success rate of 100%. Polymarket traders give the corporate supplies company a 94% chance of surpassing its estimated GAAP EPS of $1.23.

Disclosure: Polymarket odds reflect expectations, not guaranteed outcomes. The odds represent the views and expectations of traders, but actual earnings results can differ significantly from these predictions. Investors should treat Polymarket data as just one tool when evaluating their investment decisions.
